scope: Large announced or under-construction Texas datacenter anchor projects, primarily AI/HPC oriented and ≥100 MW. Operational hyperscale facilities included where they constitute a material anchor in their county.

entries.name: Project Horizon
entries.power_source: Behind-the-meter natural gas (aero-derivative turbines with SCR); air-cooled; dual long-haul fiber; 568 acres on Longfellow Ranch (Mitchell family); 8 phases of 250 MW; CoreWeave anchor lease 250 MW expandable to 500 MW.

sources.claim: 2 GW campus on 568 acres at Longfellow Ranch in Pecos County; 8 phases of 250 MW; CoreWeave anchor tenant first 250 MW with 500 MW reserved expansion; behind-the-meter aero-derivative gas turbines.
Construction completion targeted Q1 2027; CoreWeave anchor tenant 15-year lease.
Site on Mitchell-family-owned ranch in Permian Basin; self-powered design.

entries.name: Stargate Abilene (Project Ludicrous, Lancium Clean Campus)
entries.developer: Crusoe (developer); Oracle (operator); OpenAI (tenant); on Lancium Clean Campus
entries.power_source: Eight-building campus on ~1,000 acres at Lancium Clean Campus; two buildings operational since Sept 2025 with NVIDIA GB200 racks; remaining six targeted mid-2026; ERCOT-connected with on-site gas generation; 600 MW expansion lease scrapped (re-leased separately to Microsoft as adjacent campus).

sources.claim: Flagship Stargate site in Abilene, Texas; part of $500B/10 GW commitment.
1,000-acre, eight-building facility developed by Crusoe on Lancium's Clean Campus; two buildings operational on GB200 Blackwell, six more targeted mid-2026, total ~1.2 GW.
Abilene buildout could reach ~1 GW by mid-2026; Oracle ultimately deploying 450,000 GB200 GPUs under 15-year lease; first two buildings operational Sept 2025.

entries.name: Microsoft–Crusoe Abilene Campus (adjacent to Stargate)
entries.power_source: Adjacent to Stargate Abilene 1.2 GW campus on Lancium Clean Campus; two new data halls plus 900 MW behind-the-meter on-site power plant; brings combined Crusoe Abilene complex to ~2.1 GW; site originally built speculatively for Oracle/OpenAI then re-leased to Microsoft after their walk-away.

sources.claim: Crusoe adding 900 MW capacity to Abilene campus to support Microsoft AI workloads; new campus alongside 1.2 GW Oracle/OpenAI facility; on-site power plant delivering 900 MW behind-the-meter; combined site reaches 2.1 GW.
Microsoft agreed to lease up to 900 MW from Crusoe in Abilene after Oracle and OpenAI walked away from the same site; reporting variance 700–900 MW.

entries.name: Project Matador (Advanced Energy and Intelligence Campus)
entries.developer: Fermi America (Nasdaq: FRMI; Rick Perry, Toby Neugebauer co-founders); Texas Tech University System partner
entries.power_source: 5,769-acre HyperGrid campus on Texas Tech land adjacent to DOE Pantex plant near Amarillo. Behind-the-meter mix: combined-cycle natural gas (TCEQ preliminary approval Nov 2025 for first 6 GW; additional 5 GW permit filing March 2026 to upsize campus to 17 GW), four 1 GW AP1000 nuclear reactors filed with NRC, solar, battery storage. First 1 GW targeted late 2026 from gas; nuclear targeted 2032.

sources.claim: Project Matador positioned as world's largest private HyperGrid campus; ~6 of 17 GW already permitted; 7,570 acres referenced (later filing); ~2 GW of generation assets secured; built on natural gas, grid power, solar, battery, advanced nuclear; $700M+ committed financing from MUFG and others.
TCEQ preliminary approval of 6 GW gas generation announced Nov 4, 2025; 11 GW total campus; partnership with Texas Tech University System.

Site located in Carson County on Texas Tech land adjacent to Pantex; 5,769 acres; 18M sq ft data center; up to 11 GW; mix targeted at ~50% gas, 40-45% nuclear, remainder solar; three 500,000 sq ft data centers planned by end 2026 using first 1 GW.
Acquired 600+ MW of natural gas generation (Siemens SGT800 turbines via Firebird LNG); first power expected end 2026; NRC reviewing application for four Westinghouse AP1000 reactors.

entries.name: GW Ranch
entries.developer: Pacifico Energy Group (San Juan Capistrano, CA)
entries.power_source: Off-grid private-grid campus on 8,000+ acres approximately 17 miles north of Fort Stockton (one source cites 33 mi south — direction conflict noted). TCEQ air permit for 7.65 GW gas-fired generation (largest US air permit, issued Jan/Feb 2026) plus 1.8 GW battery storage and 750 MWac solar. First power H1 2027; 1 GW online 2028; 5+ GW by 2031. Independent of ERCOT.

sources.claim: Up to 7.65 GW dispatchable gas generation + 1.8 GW battery storage + up to 750 MWac solar; off-grid; first power Q1 2027; 1 GW online 2028; 5+ GW by 2031; located in Pecos County, Texas.
TCEQ approved 7.65 GW air permit Jan 2026; 8,000+ acre site approximately 17 miles north of Fort Stockton, Pecos County.
Pacifico called its 7.65 GW campus 'the largest power project in the United States'; permitted to release up to 33M tons GHG/year and 12,000+ tons regulated air pollutants/year.
Total gross capacity 7.65 GW gas + 1.8 GW BESS + 750 MWac solar; site delineations complete and turbines secured per Pacifico Jan 2026 release.

entries.name: Stargate Frontier Campus
entries.power_source: Located in southwest corner of Shackelford County. Likely the 1.4 GW campus announced by Vantage earlier in 2025; on-site natural gas via Voltagrid (TCEQ filings reference up to 210 industrial gas generators). Distinct from but co-positioned with the Abilene flagship as a central Texas Stargate hub. First building targeted 2026.

sources.claim: Shackelford County, Texas confirmed as one of three Oracle-built Stargate sites in Sept 2025 announcement, contributing to 5.5 GW combined capacity (with Doña Ana NM, a Midwest site, and the 600 MW Abilene expansion).
Site in southwest corner of Shackelford County named 'Frontier Campus'; OpenAI/Oracle direct tenants; 5,000+ construction jobs and 1,600+ permanent positions projected.
Shackelford County site likely the 1.4 GW campus announced by Vantage earlier in 2025; first building set to come online in 2026; Oracle intends to lease for AI workloads.
Voltagrid approved at Frontier Campus to operate up to 210 industrial gas generators per TCEQ public documents.

entries.name: Stargate Milam County (SoftBank/SB Energy)
entries.power_source: Central Texas site on ~595 acres ($3B campus). Grid-connected (ERCOT) with new substation buildout for accelerated power delivery; SB Energy providing powered infrastructure for fast-build site; renewable PPAs (solar/wind) integrated. Milam-plus-Lordstown OH Stargate pair scales to 1.5 GW combined over 18 months; OpenAI and SoftBank jointly invested $1B in SB Energy in Jan 2026 to accelerate development.

sources.claim: Milam County, Texas Stargate site to be developed in partnership with SB Energy (SoftBank Group); paired with Lordstown OH for 1.5 GW combined scale within 18 months.

SB Energy selected to build/operate 1.2 GW Milam County campus; initial facilities expected in service 2026; OpenAI and SoftBank invested $500M each in SB Energy (totaling $1B) in Jan 2026 to accelerate.
Milam Stargate site previously planned by SB Energy as a $3B campus spanning 595 acres; part of July 2025 OpenAI–Oracle agreement to develop 4.5 GW additional Stargate capacity.

entries.name: Meta Temple Data Center
entries.developer: Meta Platforms (developer Polmer LLC); JE Dunn (general contractor)
entries.power_source: Hyperscale campus on 393 acres at NW H K Dodgen Loop and Industrial Blvd, Temple TX. Powered via Heart of Texas Electric Cooperative grid connection; Meta-procured wind/solar PPAs (700+ MW) in Texas; ~900,000 sq ft initial buildout; design changed mid-construction (paused Dec 2022, resumed Oct 2023) to support AI workloads. Bell County tax abatement structure includes second-phase $800M expansion option.

sources.claim: Meta Temple operational hyperscale data center with 198 MW power capacity in Bell, TX; developed by JE Dunn; operational Dec 2025; connects to Heart of Texas Electric Cooperative.
$800M Meta hyperscale data center on 393 acres at NW H K Dodgen Loop and Industrial Blvd, Temple TX; ~900,000 sq ft; ~100 operational jobs; construction began Spring 2022.
Bell County tax abatement with Polmer LLC (Meta beneficial owner); 399.2-acre site at southeast corner of Industrial Blvd and Loop 363; potential additional $800M phase-two scaling.
